pages.title: About Me - Tonia Antoniazzi MP
pages.meta_description: Information about Tonia Antoniazzi, MP for Gower.
pages.text_excerpt: About Me - Tonia Antoniazzi MP TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct Tonia Antoniazzi is the Labour MP for Gower. She was first elected in 2017 and re-elected for a second term in 2019. ​In 2024, Tonia was re-elected for a third term with an increased majority of 11,567. ​ Tonia is the first woman to have been elected to represent Gower in parliament. ​Tonia and her brothers were brought up in Llanelli by her Welsh mum and Welsh-Italian father. As well as encouraging her passion for languages, they supported her love of sport and Tonia has played international rugby for Wales. After studying French and Italian at Exeter University, she received her PGCE from Cardiff University and returned home to work as a teacher. Before being elected to represent Gower, she was Head of Modern Foreign Languages in a local comprehensive school. She lives in the constituency with her son and, alongside her passion for rugby, she enjoys tennis, walking and exploring the many beautiful areas in the constituency. Since being elected Tonia has taken up a number of issues from constituents with a focus on protecting rural communities and challenging the government to keep their promises about investment in Swansea and Gower. ​ Tonia has recently been elected Chair of the Northern Ireland Affairs Committee and she is looking forward to the committee being in session. Previously she served as the Shadow Minister for Northern Ireland, as a Shadow Whip, and as Parliamentary Private Secretary to both the Shadow Chancellor and Shadow Secretary of State for Work and Pensions. Find out more about Tonia in Parliament here . pages.text_excerpt: Updates TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct Tonia Antoniazzi MP urges local groups to bid for armed forces day event funding 18/3/2026 Read More Tonia Antoniazzi backs national call for safer lorry parking on behalf of local truck drivers 16/3/2026 Read More Tonia Antoniazzi, MP for Gower, backs plan to give more rape and sexual assault survivors right to have dropped cases reviewed 30/1/2026 Read More Tonia Antoniazzi welcomes plan to protect leaseholders in Gower 28/1/2026 Tonia Antoniazzi MP has welcomed the announcement from the Labour Government that ground rents will be capped at £250 a year. She said this step is long overdue and will make a real difference to leaseholders across Gower. The Prime Minister, Keir Starmer, and the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, Steve Reed, confirmed the new cap as part of a wider package of measures designed to strengthen protections for leaseholders. Ms Antoniazzi said the change will bring much needed relief to families who have faced unfair and escalating charges for many years. Tonia Antoniazzi MP said ”I have met many constituents who own leasehold properties, and their biggest concern is they feel like they are being taken advantage of because the system lacks accountability and fairness. This cap will save some families hundreds of pounds each year and finally brings an end to one of the most unfair aspects of the leasehold system. This is especially important here in Gower, where many leaseholders have struggled with unreasonable terms and rising ground rents. Labour is delivering the change that people voted for." Tonia also welcomed the Government’s commitment to stop the creation of new leasehold flats. This delivers on Labour’s manifesto promise to bring an end to the outdated leasehold model. Tonia Antoniazzi MP added "This support for leaseholders and help with the cost of living comes after many years in which the Conservative Government failed to act. The Green Party did not mention leasehold reform in its manifesto at all. Only a Labour Government is delivering the meaningful improvements that homeowners in Gower deserve." Alongside the cap, the Government has published the draft Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill. This legislation will strengthen the rights and protections of more than five million leaseholders. The Moving to Commonhold consultation has also been launched to give residents and stakeholders the opportunity to help shape the next stage of reform. The announcement builds on the recently passed Renters Rights Act and on Labour’s planning reforms, which aim to ensure that everyone has a home that is safe, secure, and affordable. Prime Minister Keir Starmer said "This is good news for homeowners. We are capping ground rent at £250. That means that if you are a leaseholder and your current ground rent is higher than £250, you will pay less. Many people have told me this change will save them hundreds of pounds. This matters because the cost of living remains the most important issue facing people across the country. We promised that we would take this action, and I am very pleased that we are now delivering on that promise." Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government Steve Reed said "People who own flats can be forced to pay ground rents that rise to completely unaffordable levels. We said that we would support leaseholders, which is why we are introducing the ground rent cap. This will save money for millions of leaseholders and give them more control over their homes. The leasehold system has damaged the dream of home ownership for many people. We are taking action where previous governments failed by strengthening home ownership and bringing an end to leasehold." Tonia Antoniazzi MP, Labour MP for Gower, said "This is excellent

pages.text_excerpt: Updates TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct WATCH: My Speech on Removing Women from the Criminal Law on Abortion 15/4/2026 0 Comments ​During consideration of Lords amendments to the Crime and Policing Bill, I spoke in support of measures to end the criminalisation of women for abortion and to pardon those already harmed by outdated laws. Read More 0 Comments My Ten Minute Rule Bill on Police Declaration of secret societies | Full Speech, Video, and Transcript 9/3/2026 0 Comments On Wednesday 4th March I spoke to move my Ten Minute Rule Bill on Police Declaration of secret societies. ​ You can read the transcript and watch my full contribution below. ​ Read More 0 Comments My St David’s Day Debate | Full Speech, Video, and Transcript 2/3/2026 0 Comments On Thursday February 26th I spoke during the annual St David's Day debate. ​ You can read the transcript and watch my full contribution below. ​ Read More 0 Comments My amendment to decriminalise abortion in England and Wales has passed. Watch my speech in full: 18/6/2025 1 Comment On Tuesday 17th June I tabled amendment NC1 to the Crime and Policing Bill. This amendment decriminalises abortion in England and Wales and was passed with a majority of 242 votes. ​ You can read the transcript and watch my full contribution below. ​ ​Nearly five years ago, having suffered a rare complication in her abortion treatment, Nicola Packer lay down in shock, having just delivered a foetus at home. Later arriving at hospital, bleeding and utterly traumatised, she had no idea that her ordeal was about to get profoundly worse and that her life would be torn apart. Recovering from surgery, Nicola was taken from her hospital bed by uniformed police officers in a police van and arrested for illegal abortion offences. In custody, her computers and phone were seized, and she was denied timely access to vital anti-clotting medication. What followed was a four-and-a-half year pursuit by the police and the Crown Prosecution Service that completely overshadowed Nicola’s life, culminating in her being forced to endure the indignity and turmoil of a trial. She spent every penny she had funding her defence. The most private details of her life were publicly aired, and she had to relive the trauma in front of a jury—all that ultimately to be cleared and found not guilty. Nicola’s story is deplorable, but there are many others. Laura, a young mother and university student, was criminalised for an abortion forced on her by an abusive partner. He coerced her into taking abortion pills bought illegally online, rather than going to a doctor. Laura describes his violent reaction to her pregnancy: “he grabbed hold of me, pushed me against the wall, was just screaming in my face…pulling my hair and banging my head off the wall”. Laura nearly died from blood loss as a result of the illicit medication he had coerced her into taking. When she was arrested, her partner threatened to kill her if she told anyone of his involvement. Laura was jailed for two years; the partner was never investigated by the police. Another woman called an ambulance moments after giving birth prematurely, but instead of help, seven police officers arrived and searched her bins. Meanwhile, she tried to resuscitate her baby unassisted, who was Toggle showing location ofColumn 303still attached to her by the umbilical cord. While the baby was in intensive care, she was denied contact; she had to express breast milk and pass it through a door. She tested negative for abortion medication—she had never taken it. Rather, she had gone into spontaneous labour, as she had previously with her other children. She remained under investigation for a year. One of my constituents discovered that she was pregnant at seven months—she had no symptoms. She was told that she was t

pages.title: How I Can Help
headings: h2: How I Can Help
pages.text_excerpt: How I Can Help TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct How I Can Help There are many ways I can help constituents with problems or issues of concern. There is a strict Parliamentary protocol which prevents me from intervening on behalf of another MP’s constituents. To find out if I am your MP, enter your postcode here before getting in touch. Individual Issues My office helps constituents with a wide range of issues, generally I am able to help with any issue that falls under the responsibility of central government, such as problems with welfare benefits and immigration. There are some issues I am not able to help with, but my team are always on hand to offer advice on where you can find the help that you need. You can book an in person appointment with a caseworker in my office on Mondays between 2 pm and 4 pm and Wednesdays between 10 am and 12 pm. Find out more about help and support, and how to get in touch with me here . Community Organisations and Groups I work closely with local groups and agencies across Gower to help improve our community, and regularly visit local schools, businesses, and charities. My office can support groups in finding funding on either a one to one basis or at my regular funding fayres, and I have provided several groups with letters of support for grants. If you’re looking for specific support and advice, please get in touch . Policy issues Part of my role is to ensure my constituents' voices are heard in Parliament. I do this through speaking in the House of Commons about issues of concern to constituents like access to post offices in rural communities, and the cost of energy. I also regularly write to ministers and ask questions of the Government on constituents’ behalf. If you have an issue you’d like me raise please get in touch. Please note that I do not take queries on the above over social media. Please contact my office directly. Privacy policy Promoted by Hollie Ridley on behalf of the Labour Party, 20 Rushworth Street, London SE1 0SS Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct

pages.title: Get in touch
pages.meta_description: Get in touch with Tonia Antoniazzi, MP for Gower.
pages.text_excerpt: Get in touch TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct My office is not currently open for casework drop-ins. Please get in touch by phone or email if you need assistance. Address: Ty Newydd Community Centre 17 West Street Gorseinon SA4 4AA Telephone: 01792 899 025 Email: [email protected] You can also attend a one of my regular advice surgeries which are open to all constituents. The following areas are devolved to the Welsh Parliament: Health, Housing, Local Government, Education, Economic Development, Transport, Rural Affairs, Arts, Sports and Culture . ​ If your query is related to one of these issues you can get in touch with your Senedd member, Rebecca Evans MS in the following ways. Telephone: 01792 899081 Email: [email protected] There are some issues which your local councillor will be best placed to address, such as waste collections, planning applications and potholes. You can find out who your local councillor is here: ​ Your Councillors - Swansea Useful Links Swansea Council Information on local services such as waste collection, schools, and planning. Wales TUC Advice and information about your rights at work and which trade union represents your sector. Swansea University Law Clinic Offering free legal advice on a range of issues. Citizens Advice Advice on a number of issues such and benefits, housing and immigration. They can also be contacted via their local advice line 0808 278 7926 Shelter Cymru Housing advice for renters and home owners. Privacy policy Promoted by Hollie Ridley on behalf of the Labour Party, 20 Rushworth Street, London SE1 0SS Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct

pages.text_excerpt: Tonia Antoniazzi - Advice Surgeries TONIA ANTONIAZZI MP Home About Tonia News > Latest News Speeches PubAid Newsletter In Parliament How I Can Help Get in touch Advice Surgeries Wider Support Office Conduct Advice Surgeries I hold regular surgeries across the constituency throughout the year but you don't need to attend a surgery to discuss a case of raise an issue. You can contact me and my team at any time. Upcoming Surgeries Location Date Mumbles Friday 26th July If you'd like to see me at one of my surgeries, you can book an appointment by contacting my office on 01792899025 or via email at [email protected] . Constituents will receive information regarding the specific time and location of the surgery upon booking an appointment.
